cover.title: THE BLADE
cover.subtitle: The one who sees the pattern early, then gives it one more chance.
cover.tagline: You already know what matters, but hope keeps asking for one more confirmation.

profile_scores.caption: High drive and pride, low warmth. You trust slowly and judge accurately, but standards come before warmth.

short_summary: You trust slowly because you know actions matter more than nice words.
Your pattern is not missing the sign. It's seeing it early and still giving the relationship one more chance.
What changes love for you is trusting your first judgment before the explanation becomes more convincing than the behavior.

full_report.headline: THE BLADE
full_report.intro: You're not someone who trusts people quickly. You watch how they act, what they repeat, and whether they keep their promises. You often see the problem early, but you stay to confirm it one more time. This report follows that pattern: why you keep understanding what should have been enough, who pulls you into second chances, and how to trust your own judgment sooner.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're not someone who trusts people quickly.
You don't give everything from the start.
You don't say everything from the start, either.
Instead, you watch.
How they act. What they keep repeating. Whether they keep their promises. Or don't.
People sometimes read that as coldness.
But that isn't the whole picture.
You're not shutting people out. You're checking whether their actions hold up.
Because you know. Actions matter more than nice words.
So you're careful about who you choose.
But once you choose, you stay. You don't give up easily. You don't leave easily.
That's why it hurts more. You don't love just anyone.
chapters.profile_note: High Drive · High Pride, Low Warmth. You trust slowly and judge accurately, but standards come before warmth.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat
body: This pattern may feel strange.
You're pretty good at reading relationships.
You know where the problem is. You know what the other person is avoiding. You roughly know where this relationship is headed.
But even knowing that, you stay.
You may think things could change if you watch a little longer. Maybe one more conversation will make it clearer. Maybe one more chance will show something different.
So time passes.
And sometimes, the problem you noticed early is still there when you finally look again.
That moment is the emptiest.
Not because you didn't know. But because you knew from the beginning.
chapters.highlight: The pattern isn't that you miss the sign. It's that hope keeps asking for one more confirmation.

chart.title: THE SECOND CHANCE CURVE
chart.description: The pattern of giving the relationship one more chance even after spotting the problem.

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.belief: I have no eye for people.
items.truth: You may see the sign earlier than you think. The harder part is deciding when what you saw is enough to matter.
items.truth_short: You may see the sign early. The harder part is trusting that it matters.

items.belief: This time I really didn't know.
items.truth: Think back to the first moment something felt off. That feeling was information, even if you were not ready to decide what it meant yet.
items.truth_short: The first uneasy moment was information, even before you knew what it meant.

items.belief: Why do I keep making the same mistake?
items.truth: You may not be repeating the exact same mistake. You may be repeating the need to check whether this time will be different.
items.truth_short: The repeated part may be the need to check one more time.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You're not drawn to someone perfect from the start.
You're drawn to someone who needs understanding.
Someone with a reason. A cause. An explanation that makes sense.
Because you're someone who sees context.
The part to watch is this.
A behavior can make sense and still leave you hurt. Understanding the reason does not automatically make the behavior okay.
chapters.highlight: Someone who wants another chance gives a reason. Someone who values the relationship also changes what they do.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't more analysis.
You already do plenty of that.
What you need is more trust in your own judgment.
Trust in your own judgment.
The first moment something felt off. The first repeated gap. The first time the behavior and the explanation did not match.
You can treat those moments as information instead of talking yourself out of them.
They may not tell you everything, but they can still show you where a closer look is needed.
chapters.action_sentence: Treat the first repeated gap as information, then look for what happens next.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You know how to understand a relationship.
You know how to read a situation.
You know how to read a person.
But what you'll learn next is how to tell the difference between an explanation and a change.
Anyone can give a reason.
But not everyone actually changes.
Once you keep that difference in view, the relationship becomes easier to read.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time learning how to understand people. The next step is trusting what repeated behavior shows you.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.description: You hear about what they've survived before you fully know who they are now. Their pain may explain some of their behavior, but it does not decide what you have to accept.
The conversations can last for hours. Every decision has a reason. But when the same issue returns, a clear explanation still needs to be followed by something different.

items.type_title: Someone Whose Potential Is Easy to See
items.description: You can see who they might become more clearly than who they are today. The future feels hopeful, but the relationship still has to be lived in the present.

items.type_title: Someone Who Knows How to Apologize
items.description: They may sound sincere and understand what went wrong. That matters, but repair also needs to show up in what happens next.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: Many of these signs may show up early. A convincing reason can make them easier to overlook.

signal_strength.label: There's a small gap between what they say and what they do
There are moments they treat promises lightly
There's always an explanation, but never any change

chapters.core_signal: You already understand the problem, but the explanation keeps getting more attention than the repeated behavior.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: Action comes before words.
checklist.description: Their actions begin to support what they say.

checklist.title: Mistakes don't repeat.
checklist.description: A mistake can happen. What matters is whether the same pattern keeps returning without repair.

checklist.title: They don't have to prove trust.
checklist.description: Trust grows through consistent action, not constant persuasion.

checklist.title: Your mind feels at ease.
checklist.description: This matters. Your mind does not have to stay busy explaining the same gap again and again.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You're vulnerable to someone whose explanations are easier to trust than their repeated behavior. The reason may be thoughtful. The backstory may make sense. But when nothing changes, understanding can keep you in a relationship that still hurts.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier match is someone who repairs what they can, keeps promises more often than not, and lets their actions carry the apology. Around someone like this, your mind does not have to keep checking the same issue.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to someone whose story makes you want to give one more chance. That attraction does not decide what happens next. The difference is whether the new chance comes with new behavior, not only a better explanation.
sections.next_signs: This time, the pattern may show up in smaller gaps. A promise is treated lightly. The same issue returns. The explanation sounds familiar. Notice whether the behavior begins to move too.
sections.try_this: When something feels off, write down what happened before listening to the explanation. Then give both pieces equal weight. Over time, look for whether the behavior changes, not whether the reason becomes more convincing.
sections.final_line: What matters is not one reaction. It is whether words and behavior start moving closer together.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to read people. What's left is learning to trust what you saw before the explanation talks you out of it.
action_items: When something feels off, write down the behavior before deciding what the explanation means.
Treat repeated explanations without changed behavior as information.
Before giving another chance, ask whether anything has actually changed.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to understand people. The next report covers why you keep passing over signs you already recognize, and how you can start trusting that judgment.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
